message:
fix: increase LLM request timeout from 120s to 10min for agentic workloads

Reasoning models (codex 5.2 with xhigh effort) routinely exceed the
120-second default request timeout, causing "context deadline exceeded"
errors that kill the entire session. Two of the first three benchmark
failures were caused by this timeout, not model capability.

The Session now explicitly sets AdapterTimeout with a 10-minute request
limit, appropriate for agentic workloads where deep reasoning is
expected.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -1112,6 +1112,11 @@ func (s *Session) processOneInput(ctx context.Context, input string) (string, er
 			Tools:      s.allToolDefinitions(),
 			ToolChoice: &llm.ToolChoice{Mode: "auto"},
 			WebSearch:  true,
+			AdapterTimeout: &llm.AdapterTimeout{
+				Connect:    10 * time.Second,
+				Request:    10 * time.Minute,
+				StreamRead: 30 * time.Second,
+			},
 		}
 		if opts := s.profile.ProviderOptions(); opts != nil {
 			req.ProviderOptions = opts
